name: martix-sharepoint-spfx

publisher.name: MartiXDev
publisher.distributionModel: standalone skill with optional direct Copilot marketplace registration

summary: Standalone-first MartiX skill package for SharePoint Framework client-side development across SharePoint Online, Teams-connected, and Viva-connected SPFx scenarios.
abstract: This package is the canonical source for the MartiX SharePoint Framework skill. It ships authored entrypoints, 11 high-signal rule files across 10 domains, 10 reference maps, templates, taxonomy assets, a host-support matrix, and eval seeds for SharePoint Online-first SPFx work while explicitly excluding classic farm-solution development.

languageBaselines: SharePoint Server 2019 or Subscription Edition compatibility notes

status.scaffoldNote: The package is fully authored for its initial preview scope: 11 SPFx rule files across 10 domains, 10 reference docs, local markdownlint config, taxonomy assets, a host-support matrix, install-focused entrypoints, and eval scaffolding are in place. Shared marketplace registration is handled separately from this package.

notes: Official docs currently show npx skills add, not npx skill add.
Prefer a direct folder path or direct GitHub tree URL because skills is not a documented repo-root discovery location.

secondarySurface.strategy: register the canonical standalone package directly from skills through repository-level shared metadata

distribution.note: This folder is the canonical standalone package source. Shared marketplace registration is managed outside the package and can point here without a duplicate mirror.

precedenceModel: Use current SharePoint developer TOC lanes and SPFx-specific Microsoft Learn pages first for surface selection, toolchain, packaging, and host-integration guidance.
Treat SharePoint Online as the default SPFx target and use current compatibility tables for exact Node, TypeScript, and React guidance.
Use SharePoint Server 2019 and Subscription Edition pages only as compatibility constraints, not as the default implementation baseline for modern client-side work.
When current official docs disagree about Subscription Edition support, keep the answer conservative and name the ambiguity explicitly instead of flattening it.
Route classic farm-solution topics to separate server-side SharePoint guidance and provisioning or automation topics to separate provisioning guidance.

rulePrefixes.foundation: SharePoint Online-first scope checks, host boundaries, classic exclusions, and conservative support notes
rulePrefixes.tooling: SPFx version compatibility, Node or React alignment, Heft versus gulp, scaffolding, serve, and debug posture
rulePrefixes.webparts: Client-side web parts, property panes, propertiesMetadata, reactive versus non-reactive configuration, and page-hosted experiences
rulePrefixes.extensions: Application Customizers, Command Sets, Field Customizers, placeholders, and extension-specific surface selection
rulePrefixes.form-libraries: Form Customizers, list or library form lifecycle, content-type associations, and library component reuse
rulePrefixes.data: SPHttpClient, PnPjs, MSGraphClientV3, AadHttpClient, permission requests, and runtime API boundaries
rulePrefixes.deployment: package-solution.json, feature packaging, asset hosting, app catalog rollout, and skipFeatureDeployment posture
rulePrefixes.hosts: Teams supportedHosts, Teams packaging choices, messaging-extension boundaries, Viva Connections, and ACE-related handoffs
rulePrefixes.ui: React package posture, Fluent or Office UI Fabric integration, theme tokens, CSS isolation, and theme-aware UI design
rulePrefixes.migration: Script Editor, Content Editor, JSLink, UserCustomAction, ECB, JSOM, and classic-to-modern SPFx translation

contextStrategy: Open only the routed rule or reference file for the requested task.
Use README.md for install and validation guidance.
